steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content:
Add $1$ to both sides of $$x^2+2x=72$$ so the left side becomes a perfect square.
$$x^2+2x+1=72+1$$
$$(x+1)^2=73$$

steps.title: Find the positive solution
steps.content:
Take square roots, then subtract $1$.
$$x+1=\pm\sqrt{73}$$
$$x=-1\pm\sqrt{73}$$
The positive solution uses the plus sign.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graph the equation and read the positive intersection of the two sides. It corresponds to $$-1+\sqrt{73}$$ The answer is C.
